bun run lint passes with --error-on-warnings
bun run typecheck passes (tsc --noEmit, clean)
Following .claude/commands/release.md step-by-step no longer fails on a missing `bun run version:bump` script
grep -rn 'content-type.*application/json[^;]' src/preview/proxy.ts returns no matches (every response-side content-type carries `; charset=utf-8`)
Preview proxy 401/4xx responses still carry the existing `error.code` / `error.message` / `error.hint` envelope and (for 401) the `www-authenticate` header unchanged

sections.context: Warren's agent-readiness report (id 13bf3393-2e64-4ae1-84ae-b94f9afc7567) scored 53.6% (37/69 non-skipped signals), landing the repo at Level 3 (40-60%). Level 5 requires >=80% pass rate. With 13 currently-skipped signals held constant, we need >=56 passes — i.e. 19 more failing criteria converted. This plan files one child seed per cheap, in-tree win that does NOT require any external SaaS account (Sentry, PagerDuty, Mixpanel, LaunchDarkly, etc.) and does NOT add any new agent/bot (PR-review bot, CodeQL security review). Revision 2 adds OpenAPI (api_schema_docs) and a Bun __golden__ snapshot adoption seed; the latter is operator-requested and not tied to a scored criterion. After rev-2, the in-flight criteria count (with c069/0f22 closed) gives 19 readiness wins on top of the 37 baseline (56/69 = 81.2%).
sections.approach: One child seed per failing criterion (max traceability per the operator's request). Each seed encodes the exact criterion ID in its labels so the next readiness scan can verify each pass directly. Two ordering constraints are encoded via `blocks`: AGENTS.md must land before its CI validator, and bun-test coverage thresholds must land before the code-quality-metrics tracking that depends on coverage output. All work stays inside the repo — no new SaaS accounts, no new bots, no new external services. Tools added are local-only or already-free GitHub-native primitives (Dependabot config, GitHub Actions, Biome rules, npm dev-deps like knip/jscpd).

alternatives.name: Group related criteria into fewer fat seeds
alternatives.rejected_because: Operator explicitly chose max traceability — one seed per criterion so the next readiness scan can verify each pass directly.

alternatives.name: Add Sentry + CodeQL + PagerDuty for fast observability/security wins
alternatives.rejected_because: Violates the 'no external SaaS account, no external review/agent bot' constraint set by the operator.

alternatives.name: Skip cosmetic criteria like naming_consistency / cyclomatic_complexity
alternatives.rejected_because: The cheapest wins ARE the linter additions; deferring them would force harder observability work to hit Level 5 anyway.

All 20 child seeds are merged to main; their criterion-labels match the next scan's PASS entries.

sections.context:
Nightly code patrol against warren@HEAD on 2026-05-27. All quality gates (bun test / lint / typecheck / check:all) are green. The scan surfaced two correctness/consistency findings worth fixing as small, isolated PRs:

1. Inconsistent integer parsing strictness between HTTP query / env parsers. `parseNonNegativeInt` (src/server/handlers.ts:234) and `parsePositiveInt` (src/server/probe.ts:83) reject inputs whose `Number.parseInt(raw, 10)` round-trip does not equal the original string (e.g. `?foo=5abc`), but the GET /runs pagination parser `parseRunsPagination` (src/server/handlers.ts:927) and three env parsers — `parseEnvPositiveInt` (src/preview/eviction.ts:122), `resolvePgPoolMax` (src/server/main.ts:582), and `parseIntEnv` (src/server/main.ts:670) — do NOT apply the strict round-trip check. The net effect: `GET /runs?limit=5abc` is silently accepted as `limit=5`, and `WARREN_DB_POOL_MAX=10x` / `WARREN_PAUSE_DETECTOR_TICK_MS=15000abc` silently parse to the leading integer instead of erroring. server.test.ts only covers `?limit=0` and `?limit=501`; no test covers junk-suffix inputs.

2. `readJsonBody` and `readJsonBodyOrEmpty` (src/server/handlers.ts:166-200) are 95% duplicate — same parse, same validation, same error messages, differing only in the empty-body branch. A trivial delegation collapses one into the other without changing wire behaviour.
sections.behavior_invariant: All wire-visible behaviour stays equal. Valid integer inputs (`?limit=10`, `WARREN_DB_POOL_MAX=10`) keep parsing to the same numeric value. Empty / null inputs keep their current defaults. The only OBSERVABLE change is that junk-suffix inputs that today silently coerce to the leading integer (e.g. `?limit=5abc` → 5, `WARREN_DB_POOL_MAX=10x` → 10) must now reject with the same ValidationError shape the strict parsers already use (`?<label> must be a positive integer; got '<raw>'` or equivalent). For the readJsonBody dedup, every existing handler path that calls either function must produce byte-identical responses for both happy-path and error-path inputs (empty body, non-JSON body, JSON array body, JSON null, JSON object) — the error strings emitted by the two functions today are already identical, so this must remain true. No public API signature changes; no new exports; no dependency changes.
sections.approach: Two independent narrow PRs, each landing as a single child seed. (1) Tighten the loose integer parsers to apply the strict round-trip check (`String(n) !== raw`) that the canonical parsers already use, mirroring `parseNonNegativeInt` / `parsePositiveInt`. Reject junk-suffix input with a ValidationError matching the surrounding helper's phrasing. Add focused unit / handler tests for the rejection cases. (2) Collapse `readJsonBody` into a thin wrapper that calls `readJsonBodyOrEmpty` and throws the empty-body ValidationError when the result is null — pure dedup, identical error messages preserved verbatim.

steps.title: Tighten integer parsing strictness in HTTP query + env parsers
steps.description:
Make these four loose integer parsers reject junk-suffix input the same way `parseNonNegativeInt` (src/server/handlers.ts:234) and `parsePositiveInt` (src/server/probe.ts:83) already do — by adding `String(n) !== raw` to the validation predicate (after trim/empty handling, where relevant) and throwing the same ValidationError shape the surrounding file uses:

- `parseRunsPagination` in src/server/handlers.ts:927-947 — both the limit and offset branches. Today `Number.parseInt('5abc', 10) === 5` slips through. Error phrasing must match the existing `?limit must be a positive integer` / `?offset must be a non-negative integer` strings (just extend the predicate; keep the messages).
- `parseEnvPositiveInt` in src/preview/eviction.ts:122-130 — note this trims first, so compare against the trimmed value, not the untrimmed raw. Keep the existing `ValidationError(\`${name} must be a positive integer (got ${JSON.stringify(raw)})\`)` message.
- `resolvePgPoolMax` in src/server/main.ts:580-591 — same shape; preserve the existing `Error` (not `ValidationError`) phrasing.
- `parseIntEnv` in src/server/main.ts:669-673 — currently silently falls back to the default on any non-positive parse; per its callers (`WARREN_PAUSE_DETECTOR_TICK_MS`) this is a soft parser, so the safer change here is to also reject junk-suffix instead of silently coercing. If preserving the silent-fallback contract is preferable, tighten the predicate to `Number.isFinite(n) && n > 0 && String(n) === raw` so silent fallback covers junk too. Make a deliberate call in the PR description and document it in the function's JSDoc.

Add tests:
- src/server/server.test.ts (or src/server/handlers.test.ts) — extend the `?limit=0` / `?limit=501` cases at line 352-360 with `?limit=5abc`, `?limit=10 `, `?offset=3foo` and assert the same 400 ValidationError response shape.
- src/preview/eviction.test.ts — add a junk-suffix env input case to the existing `parseEnvPositiveInt`-adjacent tests.
- src/server/main.test.ts (or wherever `resolvePgPoolMax` is reached) — add a junk-suffix env input case.

No signature changes. No new exports. Run `bun run check:all` before opening the PR.

acceptance: `bun run check:all` is green on the merged main after both fix steps land (tests, lint, typecheck, file-size guard, debt-marker guard, deps, bundle-size, gen:docs).
New unit / handler tests cover the junk-suffix rejection path for `parseRunsPagination`, `parseEnvPositiveInt`, and `resolvePgPoolMax` — and fail when the strict check is reverted.

Grep audit: every existing error string emitted by `readJsonBody` and `readJsonBodyOrEmpty` (`request body is empty; expected a JSON object`, `request body must be JSON: ...`, `request body must be a JSON object`) is still emitted byte-identically by at least one call site, verifiable by `grep` after the dedup.
No public function signatures change in src/server/handlers.ts, src/server/main.ts, src/server/probe.ts, or src/preview/eviction.ts. `bun run check:deps` (knip) reports no new unused exports.

sections.context: API consumers parsing /runs/<id>/status (or other unmatched paths under an API prefix) expect application/json. When the path doesn't match any registered route, dispatch falls through to the SPA UI handler, which has its own isApiPath guard that returns JSON — but the guard lives downstream of the UI handler invocation and the envelope differs from the route-handler NotFoundError shape. Any future drift between API_PREFIXES and matchRoute coverage risks leaking HTML to API callers. We want a single chokepoint: any path under an API prefix returns JSON, full stop.
sections.reproduction:
1. Boot warren with the UI dist built (or use the test server harness in src/server/server.test.ts).
2. GET /runs/does-not-exist/status (path is under the /runs API prefix but matches no route in the table — the registered pattern is /runs/:id, not /runs/:id/status).
3. Observe: the request currently reaches createUiHandler in src/server/ui.ts; the in-handler isApiPath guard returns a JSON 404, but only because of a second layer of defense. If isApiPath and API_PREFIXES drift from matchRoute, raw HTML leaks. Expected: dispatch in src/server/server.ts returns JSON 404 before ever invoking the UI handler, using renderError(notFound(...)).
sections.root_cause: src/server/server.ts:191-246 dispatches unmatched GETs to a uiFallback (createUiHandler). The UI handler in src/server/ui.ts:56-82 then re-checks isApiPath and returns its own JSON 404 envelope. The guard is in the wrong layer — it lives inside the UI handler, so it depends on the UI fallback always being installed and on the handler's own envelope, rather than reusing renderError(notFound(...)) from src/server/errors.ts. The fix is to lift the isApiPath check into server.ts dispatch so API-prefix paths are guaranteed to produce the canonical JSON 404 envelope regardless of UI handler state.
sections.approach: Add an isApiPath short-circuit in src/server/server.ts immediately after matchRoute fails and before the uiFallback branch: if isApiPath(url.pathname) is true, render notFound(url.pathname) and return JSON. Remove the now-redundant isApiPath guard from createUiHandler so there is exactly one place that decides 'API path → JSON 404'. Add a regression test asserting GET /runs/<id>/status returns content-type application/json with the canonical not_found envelope. Touches 3 files: server.ts, ui.ts, server.test.ts. No public API changes, no dependency changes.

steps.title: Lift API-prefix JSON-404 guard into dispatch in src/server/server.ts
steps.description: In src/server/server.ts, in the dispatch function around lines 213-246 (after `const match = matchRoute(...)` falls through and before the uiFallback branch), add: if (isApiPath(url.pathname)) { const rendered = notFound(url.pathname); return jsonResponse(rendered.status, rendered.envelope); }. Import isApiPath from ./handlers.ts and notFound from ./errors.ts if not already imported. This ensures any unmatched path under an API prefix (/runs, /agents, /projects, etc.) returns the canonical JSON not_found envelope via renderError-style shape, never reaching the SPA fallback. Keep the existing uiFallback branch for non-API GETs (deep links).

steps.title: Remove redundant isApiPath guard from createUiHandler in src/server/ui.ts
steps.description: In src/server/ui.ts around line 63, delete the line `if (isApiPath(requested)) return notFoundResponse(requested);` and remove the now-unused import of isApiPath from ./handlers.ts. Also remove the now-unused notFoundResponse helper (lines ~132-140) if it has no other callers (grep confirms). The UI handler should only ever be invoked for non-API GETs after step 1; if a non-API path doesn't exist under dist, the SPA index.html fallback remains correct.

steps.title: Add regression test for JSON 404 on unmatched API-prefix path
steps.description: In src/server/server.test.ts, add a test under the existing dispatch describe block: 'returns JSON 404 with not_found envelope for unmatched path under /runs prefix'. Use the existing server harness to GET /runs/does-not-exist/status. Assert: response.status === 404; response.headers.get('content-type') starts with 'application/json'; parsed body shape is { error: { code: 'not_found', message: <string containing the pathname> } }. Add a second assertion that the response body is NOT the SPA index.html shell (e.g. does not contain '<!DOCTYPE html').

steps.title: Release: run /release per .claude/commands/release.md
steps.description: Run the release command per .claude/commands/release.md to ship the fix. Ensure quality gates (bun test, bun run lint, bun run typecheck, bun run check:all) pass before release.

acceptance: GET to an unmatched path under any API prefix (e.g. /runs/<id>/status, /agents/<x>/whatever) returns HTTP 404 with content-type application/json and a body matching { error: { code: 'not_found', message: <string> } }.
createUiHandler in src/server/ui.ts no longer imports or references isApiPath; the only isApiPath caller is server.ts dispatch.
Regression test in src/server/server.test.ts asserts JSON 404 (not HTML) for unmatched API-prefix paths and passes under `bun test src/server/server.test.ts`.
Existing deep-link tests (GET /projects/<id>, GET /runs/<id> rendering the SPA shell) still pass — non-API GETs continue to fall through to the SPA.
bun run check:all passes.

name: Return JSON 404 for unknown API paths instead of SPA HTML

sections.context: The CI-postgres workflow (.github/workflows/ci-postgres.yml) fails on every PR with 'relation "public.projects" does not exist'. 138 postgres-dialect tests cannot run, so the pg branch of the dialect-polymorphic repo layer is effectively unverified on PRs. Per warren-a540 evidence (run_9vc128s4zhfx, run_587z0gsqbnpk), the failure is identical across branches and CI-postgres has never run green on main since the workflow was introduced. Postgres tests use a per-test schema (warren_test_<8hex>) via src/db/testing.ts and rely on search_path; FK references hard-coded to "public"."projects" cannot resolve because projects lives in the test schema, not public.
sections.reproduction:
1. Run `WARREN_TEST_DIALECT=postgres WARREN_TEST_PG_URL=postgres://... bun test src/db/migrate.test.ts` (or any pg-dialect test) — or watch any PR's ci-postgres job.
2. Observe migration failure: `ALTER TABLE "agents" ADD CONSTRAINT "agents_project_id_projects_id_fk" FOREIGN KEY ("project_id") REFERENCES "public"."projects"("id") ON DELETE cascade ON UPDATE no action;` → `error: relation "public.projects" does not exist`.
3. Expected: migrations apply cleanly into the per-test schema; FK references resolve via search_path (which testing.ts sets to the test schema).
4. Confirm: `grep -rn '"public"\.' src/db/migrations/postgres/` lists 4 hard-qualified references (0004_magical_valeria_richards.sql:5; 0008_add_plan_runs.sql:34, 35, 36) — every other migration in the folder uses unqualified table names (see 0000_init.sql:74-75 for the correct pattern).
sections.root_cause: Drizzle-kit's generator emitted FK target references with an explicit "public"."<table>" qualifier when the snapshot pinned a schema in 0004_magical_valeria_richards.sql and 0008_add_plan_runs.sql. The earlier 0000_init.sql uses unqualified "projects"("id") and works fine under any search_path. The test substrate in src/db/testing.ts:152-174 creates a fresh schema warren_test_<hex>, pins search_path + drizzle's migrationsSchema to it, and runs migrations — so unqualified references resolve to the test schema, but "public"."projects" looks in the public schema where no projects table exists for that test run. Drizzle-kit's config (drizzle.config.postgres.ts) has no schemaFilter and the generator chose to emit qualified references; removing the qualifier matches the production-deployment behavior (search_path defaults to public there, so unqualified == public.<table>) and unblocks the test substrate. The 4 offending lines have not been applied in any production deployment of warren postgres (postgres support is opt-in V1; CI-postgres has never been green on main), so editing the migration SQL in place is safe and does not require a corrective migration.
sections.approach: Edit the 4 offending lines in src/db/migrations/postgres/0004_magical_valeria_richards.sql (line 5) and src/db/migrations/postgres/0008_add_plan_runs.sql (lines 34, 35, 36) to drop the `"public".` schema qualifier from FK REFERENCES targets. Match the unqualified style already used by 0000_init.sql:74-75 (and every other FK in the folder). Do NOT touch _journal.json or snapshots — drizzle's runtime migrator hashes SQL at apply time and stores it in __drizzle_migrations; since no prod deployment has applied these migrations (per bug evidence), there is no existing-hash conflict. Verify by running `WARREN_TEST_DIALECT=postgres WARREN_TEST_PG_URL=... bun test` locally if Postgres is available, otherwise rely on the CI-postgres job turning green. Alternatives rejected: (a) adding a new corrective migration that drops + re-adds the FK constraints unqualified — over-engineered for an unshipped path; (b) changing src/db/testing.ts to alias public → test schema — fragile, doesn't address production-style deployments that might one day use a non-public schema. Edit-in-place is the smallest correct intervention.

steps.title: Strip "public". qualifier from FK target in src/db/migrations/postgres/0004_magical_valeria_richards.sql
steps.description: Edit src/db/migrations/postgres/0004_magical_valeria_richards.sql line 5: change `REFERENCES "public"."projects"("id")` to `REFERENCES "projects"("id")`. Leave the rest of the file (line numbers 1-4 and 6+) untouched. Match the unqualified style used by 0000_init.sql lines 74-75. Do not touch src/db/migrations/postgres/meta/_journal.json or any snapshot file.

steps.title: Strip "public". qualifier from 3 FK targets in src/db/migrations/postgres/0008_add_plan_runs.sql
steps.description: Edit src/db/migrations/postgres/0008_add_plan_runs.sql lines 34, 35, 36: change `REFERENCES "public"."plan_runs"("id")`, `REFERENCES "public"."runs"("id")`, and `REFERENCES "public"."projects"("id")` to the unqualified `REFERENCES "plan_runs"("id")`, `REFERENCES "runs"("id")`, and `REFERENCES "projects"("id")` respectively. Do not touch any other lines in the file. Confirm with `grep -n '"public"\.' src/db/migrations/postgres/0008_add_plan_runs.sql` that no matches remain.

steps.title: Verify no "public". qualifiers remain and run pg tests if available
steps.description: Run `grep -rn '"public"\.' src/db/migrations/postgres/` and confirm zero matches. If a Postgres instance is reachable, run `WARREN_TEST_DIALECT=postgres WARREN_TEST_PG_URL=postgres://... bun test src/db/` to confirm migrations apply cleanly and pg-dialect tests pass. If no local Postgres is available, document this in the commit message and rely on the CI-postgres workflow to turn green on the PR.

steps.title: Release: run /release per .claude/commands/release.md
steps.description: Run the release command per .claude/commands/release.md. Confirm CI-postgres turns green on the PR (per acceptance criteria) before merge.

acceptance: `grep -rn '\"public\"\\.' src/db/migrations/postgres/` returns zero matches.
CI-postgres workflow (ci-postgres.yml) runs to completion and `bun test` exits 0 on the PR branch — no 'relation "public.projects" does not exist' errors.
The 138 previously-failing postgres-dialect tests now execute (whether they pass is a separate concern; this bug is about being able to apply migrations at all).
No changes to src/db/migrations/postgres/meta/_journal.json or any snapshot file.
bun run check:all passes (sqlite path unchanged).

name: Drop hard-coded public schema qualifier from postgres FK migrations

sections.context: Run run_s7vhgvkvc1v6 (2026-05-27) was triggered by a plan-run, hit an Anthropic overloaded_error (529) inside 7 seconds, produced zero model output, but warren finalized the run as `succeeded`. The plan-run machinery then opened a PR with stale branch state, waited for merge, and advanced — propagating a false-success into downstream automation. Any future provider outage (overloaded, rate limit, network) will produce the same silent-success failure mode. Plan-run authors and operators relying on run outcomes need agent_end-with-error to surface as failed so reap's failure-reason inference and downstream gates kick in.
sections.reproduction:
1. Trigger any pi-runtime run while Anthropic returns a 529 overloaded_error (or stub the pi agent_end event payload in src/runs/stream.test.ts to mirror the observed shape: { type: 'agent_end', stopReason: 'error', errorMessage: '{"type":"error","error":{"type":"overloaded_error","message":"Overloaded"}}', usage: { input: 0, output: 0, totalTokens: 0 }, content: [], willRetry: true }).
2. Observe: detectRuntimeTerminal in src/runs/stream.ts:508 returns 'succeeded' because `env.type === 'agent_end'` is the only check; the error signals in the payload are ignored.
3. Bridge calls reap with outcome='succeeded'; reap.ts:902-905 only invokes inferFailureReason when outcome === 'failed', so the run finalizes as succeeded with zero events of useful work.
4. Expected: detectRuntimeTerminal returns 'failed' for this payload, reap transitions the run to failed, and inferFailureReason populates failure_reason.
sections.root_cause: src/runs/stream.ts detectRuntimeTerminal (lines 501-510) maps `agent_end` → 'succeeded' unconditionally: `if (env.type === 'agent_end') return 'succeeded';`. The claude-code path one line above (`env.type === 'result'`) inspects `env.is_error` and returns 'failed' when true; the pi path has no equivalent guard. Pi's agent_end envelope carries clear error signals (stopReason === 'error', non-empty errorMessage, zero-usage, empty content array) that the function never inspects. Because the bridge hands 'succeeded' to reap, reap.ts:904 skips inferFailureReason and the run records as succeeded despite zero model work.
sections.approach: Extend detectRuntimeTerminal in src/runs/stream.ts to inspect the pi agent_end payload for error signals and return 'failed' when any are present. Authoritative signals, in order of preference: (1) `stopReason === 'error'` on the envelope, (2) non-empty string `errorMessage`. These are emitted by burrow's pi parser whenever pi reports an error condition. Do NOT use zero-tokens or empty-content alone as a failure signal — a legitimately quick noop run could share that shape; require an explicit error marker. Mirror the existing claude-code pattern (`env.is_error === true ? 'failed' : 'succeeded'`) for symmetry. Add a focused unit test in src/runs/stream.test.ts covering the overloaded_error payload and a happy-path agent_end (no error signals) to lock in the discriminator. Skip the optional reap-side safety net suggested in the bug description (downgrade succeeded → failed when no stdout/thinking/tool_use events were observed) — that would touch reap.ts + reap.test.ts in addition to stream.ts + stream.test.ts (4 files) and is a separate, broader hardening. File a follow-up if a second incident proves the runtime-side fix isn't sufficient.

steps.title: Inspect pi agent_end payload for error signals in detectRuntimeTerminal
steps.description: In src/runs/stream.ts, modify detectRuntimeTerminal (around lines 501-510). For the `env.type === 'agent_end'` branch, before returning 'succeeded', check: (a) `typeof env.stopReason === 'string' && env.stopReason === 'error'` → return 'failed'; (b) `typeof env.errorMessage === 'string' && env.errorMessage.length > 0` → return 'failed'. Otherwise return 'succeeded' as today. Mirror the existing claude-code `result` branch one line above for stylistic consistency. Add a short inline comment citing warren-e281 explaining why agent_end can carry an error envelope (pi runtime exits with stopReason='error' and a serialized provider error in errorMessage when the underlying API errors mid-stream, e.g. Anthropic 529 overloaded_error). Do not touch isPiAgentEnd (lines ~520-535) — its purpose is the piStats snapshot trigger, which should still fire on any agent_end (success or failure) so the final usage record gets fetched.

steps.title: Add stream.test.ts coverage for agent_end-with-error and agent_end-success
steps.description: In src/runs/stream.test.ts, add a describe block (or extend an existing one) for detectRuntimeTerminal. Add two tests: (1) 'agent_end with stopReason=error returns failed' — construct a RunEvent with kind='state_change', stream='system', payload={ type: 'agent_end', stopReason: 'error', errorMessage: '{"type":"error","error":{"type":"overloaded_error","message":"Overloaded"}}', usage: { input: 0, output: 0, totalTokens: 0 }, content: [], willRetry: true }, assert detectRuntimeTerminal returns 'failed'. (2) 'agent_end without error signals returns succeeded' — same shape but stopReason='end_turn' (or omitted) and no errorMessage, assert returns 'succeeded'. If detectRuntimeTerminal is not exported, export it for testing or add a thin test-only re-export alongside the existing seam pattern in stream.ts (check how stream.test.ts already accesses internals before adding new exports). Also add a regression assertion that an agent_end-with-error event does NOT cause isPiAgentEnd to return false — the piStats trigger should still fire.

steps.title: Release: run /release per .claude/commands/release.md
steps.description: Run the release command per .claude/commands/release.md. Confirm bun run check:all passes before release.

acceptance: detectRuntimeTerminal in src/runs/stream.ts returns 'failed' for any agent_end event whose payload has stopReason === 'error' OR a non-empty errorMessage.
detectRuntimeTerminal still returns 'succeeded' for agent_end events with no error signals (regression-locked by a test).
isPiAgentEnd still returns true for agent_end events regardless of error state, so the piStats snapshot trigger continues to fire.
New tests in src/runs/stream.test.ts pass under `bun test src/runs/stream.test.ts`.
bun run check:all passes.

name: Map pi agent_end with error payload to failed in detectRuntimeTerminal

sections.context: Of the last 30 PRs (#122–#151), 10 had failing first-pass CI runs. Every failure falls into two classes that share the same shape: the local quality gate the agent ran accepted work that CI then rejected. Class A (10 PRs) is the ci-postgres FK qualifier ("public".X) — never executed by `bun run check:all` because there is no local postgres test matrix, so the migration that aborts in CI passes locally. Class B (PR #151) is `validate:agents-md` flagging gitignored build artifacts; this IS in check:all but the agent skipped the gate and committed anyway. The substrate already exists (warren-5797 landed `qualityGate` in `.warren/config.yaml`, `WARREN_QUALITY_GATE` env injection in `composeRunEnv()`, and the `scripts/hooks/pre-commit` hook), but parity has drifted: pre-commit runs a strict subset of check:all, check:all runs a strict subset of CI (missing `check:duplicates` and the entire ci-postgres workflow), `.warren/config.yaml` for this repo does not set `qualityGate`, and built-in agent prompts (claude-code/sapling/pi) describe the gate as advisory rather than a terminal condition. Net result: a diligent agent following the prompt verbatim will still ship PRs that break CI on first pass, blocking auto-merge and forcing manual intervention.
sections.approach: Collapse the three diverged gate definitions (pre-commit hook, `bun run check:all`, CI workflows) onto a single source of truth, then enforce parity structurally. Five wedges: (1) Extend `check:all` so it runs every CI step locally, including `check:duplicates` and a real `bun run test:pg` that boots a docker postgres via testcontainers-style helper and runs the matrix tests; (2) add `scripts/check-ci-parity.ts` that parses the GitHub workflow YAMLs, extracts each `bun run <X>` invocation, and fails if any step isn't reachable from `check:all` — wire into check:all itself so future CI drift breaks the local gate immediately; (3) set `qualityGate: "bun run check:all"` in this repo's `.warren/config.yaml` so deployed agents pick it up via WARREN_QUALITY_GATE without falling through to fuzzy fallback heuristics; (4) replace the pre-commit hook body with `exec bun run check:all` so a forgetful agent literally cannot commit a red tree; (5) rewrite the operating-contract block in the three coding-agent built-ins from advisory ("fix failures before committing") to terminal ("you are not done until $WARREN_QUALITY_GATE exits zero on the final commit … do not declare the task complete with a red gate"). Optional follow-on: a WARREN_QUALITY_GATE_REQUIRED=1 reap-side check that refuses to push if the last commit lacks a green-gate marker — closes the prompt-bypass loop entirely, but ship the prompt+parity wedges first.

alternatives.name: Reactive pr-fixer agent (warren-05ea): poll CI status and dispatch a fixer run when a PR fails
alternatives.rejected_because: Bandages the symptom (broken PR sitting open) rather than preventing the failure; useful as a downstream safety net but does not address the root cause.

alternatives.name: Make CI advisory rather than blocking and merge through failures
alternatives.rejected_because: Defeats the purpose of CI and lets bad migrations land in main.

alternatives.name: Ratchet down what CI runs to match check:all (drop jscpd, drop ci-postgres)
alternatives.rejected_because: Each CI step exists for a reason (jscpd catches copy-paste rot, ci-postgres catches dialect drift). Removing them shrinks coverage instead of expanding the local gate.

steps.title: Add `bun run test:pg` script + script that boots a docker postgres and runs the matrix tests with WARREN_TEST_DIALECT=postgres
Extend `check:all` to include `check:duplicates` (jscpd) so it actually mirrors ci.yml
Add `scripts/check-ci-parity.ts` drift detector: parse ci.yml + ci-postgres.yml, fail if any `bun run <X>` is not transitively reachable from `check:all`; wire into check:all itself
Set `qualityGate: "bun run check:all"` in this repo's `.warren/config.yaml` so deployed agents read it via WARREN_QUALITY_GATE instead of the fuzzy fallback
Replace `scripts/hooks/pre-commit` body with `exec bun run check:all` so a red tree cannot be committed; verify hook installs in burrow sandboxes (warren-5797 follow-through)

Rewrite the operating-contract block in claude-code / sapling / pi built-ins: green gate is terminal, not advisory — explicit "you are not done until $WARREN_QUALITY_GATE exits zero; do not declare task complete with a red gate" language
Run quality gates, validate the new check:all on a dirty migration (regression for Class A) and a missing-artifact reference (regression for Class B), open PR, confirm CI is green first-pass

Run the release flow per .claude/commands/release.md: review `git log <last-tag>..HEAD`, bump patch version in package.json + src/index.ts (must agree, release workflow enforces), move CHANGELOG.md [Unreleased] entries into the new version section with today's date, update CLAUDE.md/README.md if relevant, commit; release.yml auto-tags v$VERSION and creates the GitHub release

risks: test:pg requires Docker available in the burrow sandbox; if absent the script must fail loudly with a fix-it hint rather than skip silently (skip-silent is how parity drifts in the first place).

Pre-commit running full check:all may be slow on every commit. Mitigation: bun test is already fast (<10s); the long tail is build:ui inside check:bundle-size:build. Acceptable cost — a slow commit is cheaper than a red PR that blocks auto-merge for an hour.

Drift detector may produce false positives if CI uses workflow-only commands (e.g. `actions/upload-artifact`). Scope it to `bun run …` invocations only; allow an explicit allowlist with comments for genuine workflow-only steps.
Setting qualityGate in this repo only affects warren-on-warren runs. Other projects warren manages still need their own .warren/config.yaml. Document the convention; not a blocker.
Tightening agent prompts increases per-run cost (model re-runs gate until green). Mitigation: same as the slow-commit point — cheaper than the human-in-loop alternative.
acceptance: `bun run check:all` runs every step the GitHub workflows run; CI adds no failure mode that check:all does not.
`scripts/check-ci-parity.ts` is wired into check:all; adding a `bun run new-thing` step to ci.yml without adding it to check:all causes check:all to fail with a pointer to the missing step.
`.warren/config.yaml` sets `qualityGate: "bun run check:all"`; an agent dispatched against this repo from deployed warren observes `WARREN_QUALITY_GATE=bun run check:all` inside the burrow sandbox.
`scripts/hooks/pre-commit` invokes `bun run check:all`; an attempt to `git commit` with a failing check is rejected by the hook with non-zero exit and the failing step name surfaced.
claude-code / sapling / pi system prompts contain the new terminal-gate language; the next 5 consecutive merged PRs land first-pass-green on CI.
A regression test for Class A: a new postgres migration with `REFERENCES "public"."projects"(...)` fails `bun run check:all` locally (via test:pg) without needing CI to catch it.

A regression test for Class B: adding a backticked path to AGENTS.md that doesn't exist and isn't gitignored fails `bun run check:all` locally (via validate:agents-md, unchanged) and is structurally caught before commit (via pre-commit hook running check:all).

sections.context: warren-4553 introduced per-file (500 line) and per-function line-count guards. 48 files were pinned in scripts/file-size-budgets.json above the threshold, and 8 files were grandfathered with biome noExcessiveLinesPerFunction overrides. The largest offenders — handlers.ts (4233 lines), reap.ts (2114), proxy.ts (912) — impose high cognitive load, slow code review, and make parallel agent development harder. This plan covers the ~8 production files over 700 lines and the 8 biome function-length overrides (6 of which overlap with the production splits). Files barely above 500, acceptance test scenarios, and UI workspace files are deferred to a follow-up seed.
sections.behavior_invariant: Every existing HTTP endpoint, CLI command, and UI page must behave identically after each split. The public API surface (route paths, request/response shapes, status codes, error envelopes) is frozen — only internal module boundaries change. All existing tests must pass with assertion logic unmodified (import paths may change). The file-size ratchet in scripts/file-size-budgets.json must never increase — each step lowers or removes entries for files it touches. No new public exports are introduced; each split re-exports exactly what the original module exported from the new index.ts.
sections.approach: Module extraction into same-name subdirectories. For each monolithic .ts file: (1) create a same-name subdirectory with an index.ts that re-exports the public API — callers update only the import path suffix (.ts → /index.ts), (2) move logic into domain-scoped files inside the subdirectory, (3) split the colocated test file into matching test files, (4) lower or remove the file-size-budgets.json entry, (5) remove the biome noExcessiveLinesPerFunction override if applicable, (6) verify bun run check:all passes. For biome-only overrides on test files without oversized production counterparts (client.test.ts, refresh.test.ts), extract long test functions into smaller blocks or shared test helpers. The handlers.ts split is serialized into 3 steps because each touches the same source file; all other steps are independent and can run in parallel.

steps.title: Split handlers.ts phase 1 — extract plot + plot-plan-run handlers into src/server/handlers/plots.ts and handlers/plot-plan-runs.ts; create handlers/ directory with index.ts composing the route table; relocate handlers.plots.test.ts and handlers.plot-plan-runs.test.ts alongside their new modules; lower file-size budgets for all touched files

Split handlers.ts phase 2 — extract run + plan-run handlers into src/server/handlers/runs.ts and handlers/plan-runs.ts; extract run-related tests from handlers.test.ts into handlers/runs.test.ts; relocate handlers.plan-runs.test.ts alongside; remove biome noExcessiveLinesPerFunction override for handlers.plan-runs.test.ts; lower file-size budgets

Split handlers.ts phase 3 — extract remaining handlers (agents, projects, burrows, workers, diagnostics, preview, health) into src/server/handlers/ domain files; reduce handlers/index.ts to shared helpers + route-table composer under 500 lines; split residual handlers.test.ts and handlers.preview.test.ts into per-domain test files; lower or remove all remaining handler file-size budgets

sections.context:
An audit of the Warren UI (src/ui, Tailwind v4 + CSS-first @theme tokens, CVA/Radix shadcn-style primitives, react-query) found a competent but flat and inconsistent surface. The declared brand fonts (Inter / JetBrains Mono) are never actually loaded, so most users render system fonts. There is no brand color (--color-primary is just gray). Only colors are tokenized — radius, shadow, spacing, and z-index are raw utilities that drift. Light neutrals are achromatic while dark neutrals are blue-tinted, so the gray ramps don't match across modes, and the dark token set is physically duplicated in two CSS blocks. No @custom-variant dark is defined for v4, so dark:-prefixed status colors track OS preference instead of the manual data-theme toggle. Status state is mapped in 3+ divergent places (StateBadge, PlotStatusBadge, PlanRunStateBadge, plus an ad-hoc statusVariant() in RunDetail) with color collisions and no icons/pulse. There are no loading skeletons/spinners (bare

Loading…

repeated ~10x), no Select/Checkbox/Tooltip/Alert/EmptyState/Toast primitives (raw
sections.approach: Tighten the existing stack rather than migrate it (keep Tailwind v4 @theme, CVA, Radix, react-query). Work in dependency order from foundations up: (1) fix the token layer in index.css — self-host Inter + JetBrains Mono via @fontsource, unify the gray ramp to one cool-neutral hue family across modes, introduce a single muted-green brand/accent hue (desaturated, chroma <~0.12) matching the os-eco ecosystem, tokenize the status palette into semantic vars (--color-success/warning/info/danger/neutral), add --radius-*, tinted --shadow-*, and a --z-* scale, add @custom-variant dark to fix the toggle, collapse the duplicated dark block, and apply tabular-nums to data surfaces. (2) Upgrade existing primitives (Button/Card/Input/Textarea/Badge/Table/Dialog) with token-based hover, active/pressed feedback, deliberate transition durations, and Card elevation variants. (3) Add the missing primitives (Select, Checkbox, Spinner, Skeleton, Tooltip). (4) Collapse all status mapping into one StatusIndicator registry (state -> label + color token + icon + optional running pulse) consumed by every badge wrapper. (5) Add shared state components (Alert/Callout, EmptyState, Toast) and a formatError() util. (6) Add framer-motion as a lightweight motion layer for staggered entry, event-stream item animations, and dialog/skeleton transitions, gated behind prefers-reduced-motion. (7) Promote repeated layout recipes (PageHeader, Field, FilterPill/Toggle group) and sweep ad-hoc rounded-md border containers onto Card variants. (8) Decompose PlotDetail.tsx onto the new primitives. Each phase is independently shippable, gated by bun run check:all (lint/typecheck/coverage/file-size/duplicates/deps/bundle-size), and lands as its own child seed. The final child runs the patch release flow.

alternatives.name: Adopt shadcn CLI + components.json and regenerate primitives
alternatives.rejected_because: The primitives are already hand-authored CVA/Radix and well integrated with the oklch token layer; introducing the CLI would churn every component for no behavioral gain and fight the bespoke token system.

alternatives.name: Big-bang rewrite of the UI package
alternatives.rejected_because: High risk against a streaming, data-heavy control plane with live event flows; the repo enforces coverage/file-size/duplicate ratchets, so incremental phased PRs are safer and reviewable.

alternatives.name: Keep system fonts and skip the brand color
alternatives.rejected_because: The audit's two highest-impact, lowest-risk wins are loading real fonts and giving Warren a visual identity; skipping them leaves the core 'flat/generic' problem unsolved.

alternatives.name: CSS-only animations instead of framer-motion
alternatives.rejected_because: Staggered entry and event-stream item choreography are awkward in pure CSS for dynamically appended NDJSON events; a small motion lib keeps it declarative and reduced-motion-aware.

steps.title: Phase 1: Design token foundations in index.css (fonts, brand green, unified grays, status/radius/shadow/z tokens, dark-variant fix)
Phase 2a: Upgrade existing primitives (Button/Card/Input/Textarea/Badge/Table/Dialog) with active states, token hover, durations, Card elevation variants
Phase 2b: Add missing primitives — Select, Checkbox, Spinner, Skeleton, Tooltip
Phase 3: Unify status into a single StatusIndicator registry (state -> label/color token/icon/pulse); refactor StateBadge, PlotStatusBadge, PlanRunStateBadge, and RunDetail statusVariant() onto it
Phase 4: Shared state components — Alert/Callout, EmptyState, Toast (Radix) + formatError() util; replace inline Loading/empty/error sites
Phase 5: Motion layer — add framer-motion for staggered entry, event-stream item animations, dialog/skeleton transitions, gated behind prefers-reduced-motion
Phase 6: Layout promotions — PageHeader, Field (label+control+error), FilterPill/Toggle group; sweep ad-hoc rounded-md border containers onto Card variants
Phase 7: Decompose PlotDetail.tsx (3000+ lines) onto the new primitives and layout components, under the file-size budget
Run the patch release flow per .claude/commands/release.md (bump package.json + src/index.ts, update CHANGELOG/CLAUDE/README, run check:all, commit)

risks: Vite + React tsc -b build inside src/ui can fail with duplicate type declarations when tsc auto-discovers bun-types from the parent node_modules (mx-ef8eab). Verify the UI typecheck/build after dependency changes (framer-motion, @fontsource).
Layout's <main> wrapper must keep min-w-0 so wide tables (Runs/Projects) shrink instead of forcing horizontal scroll (mx-a8a1df). Preserve this invariant during the Phase 6 layout sweep.
Adding @custom-variant dark changes how every existing dark: utility resolves (OS preference -> data-theme). Audit all dark: usages for visual regressions across both toggle and system modes.
Self-hosted fonts add to the Vite bundle; check:bundle-size is a ratchet that only goes down — subset/limit weights to stay within budget.
Unifying the gray ramp and introducing a brand hue touches global tokens; risk of low-contrast regressions in either mode. Verify WCAG contrast for fg/bg and on-accent text.

PlotDetail decomposition risks regressing live event-stream wiring (useEventStream follow + event-driven runs invalidation, mx-84aa73 / mx-cc3a9f). Keep data/effect logic intact; only restructure presentation.
framer-motion animations must respect prefers-reduced-motion or they become an accessibility regression.
acceptance: bun run check:all passes after every phase (lint --error-on-warnings, typecheck, coverage ratchet, file-size, duplicates, deps, bundle-size, gen:docs/openapi).
Inter and JetBrains Mono are self-hosted via @fontsource and verifiably render offline (no CDN/system-font fallback for the primary faces).
A single muted-green brand/accent token exists and is used for primary buttons, active nav, links, and focus; --color-primary is no longer a bare gray.

index.css exposes a complete token set: unified gray ramp across light/dark, semantic status tokens (success/warning/info/danger/neutral), --radius-*, tinted --shadow-*, and a --z-* scale; the dark token block is no longer duplicated and @custom-variant dark makes dark: utilities track the data-theme toggle.

All run/plot/plan-run status rendering flows through one StatusIndicator registry (label + color token + icon + running pulse); the ad-hoc statusVariant() in RunDetail and the per-page status maps are gone.

No bare

Loading…

remains: async surfaces use Skeleton/Spinner; empty surfaces use a shared EmptyState; async/mutation errors surface via Alert and a global Toast; formatError() replaces the ~15 copy-pasted error expressions.
Select, Checkbox, Spinner, Skeleton, and Tooltip primitives exist and the 6+ raw <select> style-string duplicates and unstyled native checkboxes are replaced.
framer-motion drives staggered entry and event-stream item animations and is fully disabled under prefers-reduced-motion.
PlotDetail.tsx is decomposed onto the shared primitives and is within the file-size budget (no grandfather ceiling growth).
A patch release is cut per .claude/commands/release.md with package.json and src/index.ts versions in sync and CHANGELOG updated.

sections.context: Warren already ships a Cost Analytics dashboard, but the rich per-run logs (the `runs` token/cost columns plus the `events` table's `tool_use`/`tool_result` trace) are otherwise unmined. The operator wants a dedicated Run Analytics dashboard that answers behavioral questions the cost view can't: how much context (input + cache-read tokens) agents burn on average, which seeds consumed the most context, and — most importantly — where agents get stuck (commands they run repeatedly or that fail, e.g. `ml`, `sd`, `gh`/git, `bun run check:all`). Scouting confirmed the data is recoverable: per-run context lives in `runs.tokens_input/tokens_cache_read/tokens_output/cost_usd`; seed grouping works via `runs.seed_id` (seed-originated runs only); Bash commands land as `tool_use` events at `payload.input.command` with outcomes in correlated `tool_result` events (`is_error` + `content`, joined by `tool_use_id`). Known limitation: claude-code emits a single cumulative token total per run while pi emits per-turn, so per-turn growth curves are pi-only and per-run totals are the cross-runtime baseline.
sections.approach: Mirror the existing Cost Analytics architecture exactly: pure, dialect-agnostic aggregator modules under `src/runs/analytics/` (each with a co-located `*.test.ts`), thin `RouteHandler` factories under a new `src/server/handlers/runs/analytics.ts` (keeping the grandfathered `lifecycle.ts` from growing), and a TanStack-Query UI page that leans on the existing shadcn components (`Card`, `Table`, `PageHeader`) plus the `CostAnalytics.tsx` filter pattern (`useSearchParams` project + date-range). Two endpoints under the already-registered `/analytics` prefix split the work by cost: `GET /analytics/runs` reads only the `runs` table (fast) for KPIs, time-series, per-agent/per-model breakdowns and top-seeds-by-context; `GET /analytics/behavior` scans `tool_use`/`tool_result` events (heavier, capped) for generalized command mining — ranking ALL commands by frequency/failure/retry with the os-eco toolchain (ml/sd/gh/bun check:all) highlighted — and a derived insights layer. Graphs use recharts (operator-approved new dependency) for time-series and bar charts; the bundle-size ratchet is bumped once with this tracker reference. Phase 1 ships run-level metrics + context + charts; Phase 2 layers behavioral command-mining + insights callouts on top.

alternatives.name: Tables/cards only, no chart library (clone Cost Analytics verbatim)
alternatives.rejected_because: Operator explicitly wants graphs; time-series context/run trends and per-agent comparisons read far better as charts than dense tables

alternatives.name: Single combined /analytics endpoint
alternatives.rejected_because: Event-table scanning for command mining is materially heavier than reading the runs table; splitting lets the fast run-level view render immediately and the heavy behavior view load/cap independently

alternatives.name: SQL-side aggregation with json_extract in the repo
alternatives.rejected_because: json_extract semantics differ between SQLite and the optional Postgres backend; following the cost-analytics precedent of fetching rows and aggregating in a pure, unit-tested TS function keeps it dialect-agnostic and testable without HTTP

alternatives.name: Hardcode only the os-eco commands (ml/sd/gh/bun)
alternatives.rejected_because: Operator chose generalized ranking of all commands with os-eco ones highlighted, so emergent stuck-patterns outside the known toolchain are still surfaced

risks: Event-table scanning for command mining can be expensive on busy instances — cap rows per run / per query and constrain by the date-range filter, and keep /analytics/behavior separate from the fast /analytics/runs view

claude-code emits only a single cumulative token total per run (pi emits per-turn), so per-turn context-growth charts are pi-only; the dashboard must present per-run totals as the cross-runtime baseline and label pi-only views

token/cost columns are best-effort and null for agents that emit neither pi turn_end nor claude-code result — all aggregates must tolerate nulls and exclude them from averages rather than counting them as zero
runs.seed_id is null for ad-hoc (non-seed) runs, so top-seeds-by-context must exclude nulls and the UI should note it covers seed-originated runs only

Adding recharts trips check:deps/knip and check:bundle-size — recharts must be genuinely imported and the bundle-size budget bumped exactly once with this tracker reference (the ratchet otherwise only goes down)

Bash command strings may contain secrets/tokens — the behavior endpoint and UI must surface commands without leaking sensitive argument values (truncate/redact long inline tokens)
acceptance: GET /analytics/runs returns KPIs (run count, success rate, avg/median/p95 duration, avg context tokens, avg/total cost), a runs-per-day time-series bucketed by state, per-agent and per-model/provider breakdowns, a failure-reason breakdown, and top-seeds-by-context — all honoring projectId + from/to filters and tolerating null token/cost rows

The RunAnalytics page is reachable from the nav, mirrors the CostAnalytics project + date-range filter UX, and renders KPI cards plus recharts graphs (runs-over-time, avg-context-per-agent, top-seeds-by-context, failure-reason) using the existing Card/Table/PageHeader components

GET /analytics/behavior ranks all observed commands by frequency/failure/retry, correlates failures via tool_result.is_error joined on tool_use_id, flags retry loops, highlights os-eco commands (ml/sd/gh/bun run check:all), and is row-capped/date-bounded

The page surfaces a derived insights callout section (e.g. highest-context seed, worst-success agent, most-failed/most-retried command) plus a command-category chart and a stuck-command leaderboard table with os-eco highlighting
Each aggregator (run-metrics, command-mining, insights) has a co-located unit test exercising null-token, empty-input, and os-eco-highlight cases; coverage ratchet does not regress

docs/http-api.md and docs/openapi.yaml are regenerated for the two new /analytics routes, recharts is declared and the bundle-size budget updated, and bun run check:all passes clean (lint, typecheck, tests, gen:*:check, check:deps, check:bundle-size, file-size, debt-markers, duplicates)

sections.context: Code patrol of the run-analytics aggregators surfaced three correctness/consistency defects in the pure aggregators behind GET /analytics/behavior. The aggregators are well-covered by unit tests, but the tests pin each helper in isolation and miss the composed pipeline behaviour, so two command-categorization bugs and one inconsistent-denominator defect slipped through. All three are internal data-quality bugs (no API signature change, no new capability) that skew the analytics dashboard's command rankings and insight callouts.
sections.behavior_invariant: The wire shapes of GET /analytics/runs and GET /analytics/behavior stay identical (same field names, same response envelope). Existing passing assertions in command-mining.test.ts, insights.test.ts, and run-metrics.test.ts continue to hold except where they encoded the buggy behaviour. generalizeCommand/categorize remain pure and deterministic; buildInsights output ordering (severity then KIND_ORDER) is unchanged. No new fields are added to any exported interface.
sections.approach: Three small, independent fixes, each landing as its own PR with a focused regression test added to the existing co-located test file. (1) Teach generalizeBun to distinguish bun's own subcommands from run scripts. (2) Replace categorize's substring matching with token-precise matching. (3) Align worstSuccessAgent's terminal-run denominator with the rate it reports. Steps 1 and 2 both edit src/runs/analytics/command-mining.ts and its test, so step 2 is gated behind step 1 to avoid merge churn; step 3 is independent.

steps.title: Fix generalizeBun collapsing bun subcommands into 'bun run <sub>'
steps.description: src/runs/analytics/command-mining.ts:194-200 (generalizeBun). For any 'bun <x>' where x is not 'run', the helper treats x as a run-script name and returns `bun run ${x}`. This is wrong for bun's own subcommands: generalizeCommand('bun install') -> 'bun run install', 'bun add foo' -> 'bun run add', 'bun remove x' -> 'bun run remove'. The mislabelled signature then miscategorizes in categorize() as 'other' instead of 'package' (categorize('bun install') is only correct because the unit test calls it with the raw string the pipeline never produces). Fix: in generalizeBun, when the first non-flag arg is a known bun subcommand (install, add, remove, ci, uninstall, update, x, create, init, link, unlink, pm, outdated, upgrade, etc. — at minimum the package-management subcommands already in PKG_SUBS), return `bun ${sub}` (mirroring the git/gh/sd MULTI_SUBCOMMAND treatment) rather than `bun run ${sub}`. Keep 'bun run <script>', bare 'bun <script>', 'bun test', and 'bun run test' family behaviour exactly as-is. Add regression cases to command-mining.test.ts under the generalizeCommand describe block: generalizeCommand('bun install') === 'bun install', 'bun add foo' === 'bun add', and assert categorize(generalizeCommand('bun install')) === 'package'. Correct looks like: the composed generalize->categorize pipeline labels bun package operations as 'package', not 'other'.

steps.title: Fix categorize() substring matching misclassifying scripts
steps.description: src/runs/analytics/command-mining.ts:209-214 (categorize, package-manager branch). It uses generalized.includes('test') and generalized.includes('build') — substring matches against the whole generalized string. So 'bun run latest' is categorized as 'test' (because 'latest' contains 'test'), and 'bun run prebuild' / 'bun run rebuild' as 'build'. Fix: inspect the script token rather than the full string. After generalizeBun the shape is 'bun run <script>' or 'bun test'; match on the script token (e.g. parts[2] for the 'bun run <script>' form, or detect the 'bun test' signature explicitly) using equality or a token-prefix check (script === 'test' || script.startsWith('test:') ; script === 'build' || script.startsWith('build:')) instead of String.includes on the whole command. Preserve the existing correct results asserted at command-mining.test.ts:90-95 (bun install -> package once step 1 lands, bun test -> test, bun run build -> build). Add regression cases: categorize(generalizeCommand('bun run latest')) === 'other', categorize(generalizeCommand('bun run rebuild')) === 'other'. Correct looks like: only genuine test/build scripts land in the 'test'/'build' categories.

steps.title: Align worst-success-agent denominator with the rate it reports
steps.description: src/runs/analytics/insights.ts:141-157 (worstSuccessAgent). The MIN_AGENT_TERMINAL_RUNS gate (line 143) and the detail string (lines 153-154) both use `g.succeeded + g.failed` as the terminal-run count, but RunGroupBucket.successRate (src/runs/analytics/run-metrics.ts finalizeGroup) is computed as succeeded / (succeeded + failed + cancelled). When an agent has cancelled runs, the reported rate's denominator silently disagrees with the 'of N terminal run(s)' the callout prints, and the >= MIN_AGENT_TERMINAL_RUNS gate can skip (or admit) an agent based on a count that does not match the rate. RunGroupBucket does NOT expose `cancelled`, so do NOT add a field (that would change the wire shape / public interface — out of scope for this plan; the standalone seed warren-92ad tracks shape questions). Instead, make the displayed/derived count consistent with the rate that is exposed: either (a) reword the detail so it does not assert a specific terminal denominator (e.g. 'succeeded in only X% of its terminal runs'), and gate on a metric available consistently, or (b) derive the count the rate already implies. Pick the option that keeps successRate as the single source of truth and removes the succeeded+failed vs succeeded+failed+cancelled split-brain. Update insights.test.ts to cover an agent bucket with cancelled > 0 and assert the callout text no longer contradicts successRate. Correct looks like: the percentage and any count in the callout are derived from one consistent terminal definition.

name: nightwatch patrol 2026-06-01: command-mining + insights correctness fixes

sections.context: Nightwatch patrol of 2026-06-02. Lint, typecheck, and the full test suite (2444 pass / 0 fail) are all green, so the codebase is broadly healthy. The scan surfaced two small correctness/quality defects in the run-analytics layer that are safe to land as isolated PRs without touching any public signature: (1) categorize() in command-mining.ts mis-buckets colon-namespaced package-manager scripts so the project's own commands — `bun run test:ci`, `bun run test:coverage`, `bun run build:ui` — are counted as `other` instead of `test`/`build` in the behavior-analytics category rollup; and (2) the insights module and the /analytics/behavior handler docs advertise steering-anomaly and pause-anomaly callouts that can never fire because no production code ever constructs the SteeringSignals the handler would need to pass. A third finding requiring a public type change (RunGroupBucket lacking a `cancelled` field) was filed separately as warren-ec9b per patrol scope.
sections.behavior_invariant: Every existing analytics wire shape stays byte-for-byte identical except for the corrected category labels: the GET /analytics/runs and GET /analytics/behavior response envelopes keep the same keys, ordering, and types; buildCommandMining / buildInsights signatures and return types are unchanged; the only intended behavior change is that generalized package-manager commands whose script name contains a `test`/`build` colon segment (e.g. `bun run test:ci`, `bun run build:ui`) now categorize as `test`/`build`, while the existing false-positive guards (`bun run latest` -> other, `bun run rebuild` -> other) keep returning `other`. The steering/pause documentation fix changes only comments — zero runtime behavior change.
sections.approach: Two independent, comment-or-logic-local fixes plus a release step. Step 1 tightens categorize() to split each token on `:` and match exact `test`/`build` segments, then extends command-mining.test.ts with the colon-namespaced cases alongside the existing latest/rebuild guards. Step 2 corrects the doc comments in insights.ts and the analytics handler so they no longer claim steering/pause callouts are produced by the live endpoint. The steps share no code paths and can land in any order; both block the final release step.

steps.title: Fix categorize() mis-bucketing colon-namespaced package scripts in command-mining.ts
Correct steering/pause insights docs that advertise callouts the handler never wires
Release: run /release per .claude/commands/release.md.

acceptance: `bun test src/runs/analytics/command-mining.test.ts` passes with new assertions: categorize(generalizeCommand('bun run test:ci')) === 'test', categorize(generalizeCommand('bun run build:ui')) === 'build', and the existing categorize('bun run latest') === 'other' / categorize('bun run rebuild') === 'other' guards still hold.

After the doc fix, no source comment in src/runs/analytics/insights.ts or src/server/handlers/runs/analytics.ts claims GET /analytics/behavior returns steering-anomaly or pause-anomaly callouts without also noting that SteeringSignals are not currently supplied by the handler; no runtime code path changes.
`bun run check:all` is green (lint, typecheck, tests, coverage ratchet, file-size, debt-markers, duplicates, deps, bundle-size, gen:docs, gen:openapi).
No public exported type or function signature changed by either step (the RunGroupBucket.cancelled change is out of scope, tracked in warren-ec9b).

sections.context: The monolithic `src/server/handlers.ts` was long ago split into the `src/server/handlers/` directory (and parts moved to `src/server/main/`), but ~13 doc comments across the codebase still point readers to the now-nonexistent file `src/server/handlers.ts`. One reference even cites a precise dead line number (`handlers.ts:1453`). This is documentation drift: anyone following these cross-references lands on a missing file. The fix is comment-only — retarget each reference to the symbol's actual new home. No behavior changes, no API changes, no reformatting of code that passes the linter.
sections.behavior_invariant: Runtime behavior, exported symbols, and public API signatures stay byte-for-byte identical — these are comment-only edits. `bun test`, `bun run lint`, `bun run typecheck`, and `bun run check:all` must all stay green exactly as they are today. No `.ts`/`.tsx` executable line changes; only the text inside doc comments / inline comments is updated. After the sweep, `grep -rn 'handlers\.ts' src --include='*.ts' --include='*.tsx' | grep -v '.test.' | grep -v 'handlers/'` returns zero stale monolith references (every remaining `handlers/...` path is a real directory module).
sections.approach: Walk each stale reference and repoint it at the symbol's verified current location, grouping edits by file tree so each lands as one small, reviewable, comment-only PR. Verified new homes: `getBurrowHandler`/list-burrows -> `src/server/handlers/burrows.ts`; `isAuthExempt` -> `src/server/handlers/index.ts`; `loadPausedRunsForPlot` -> `src/server/handlers/plots/shared.ts`; `createRunHandler`/Run-Now dispatch -> `src/server/handlers/runs/index.ts` (+ `dispatch.ts`); `POST /agents/refresh` -> `src/server/handlers/agents.ts`; `plan_run.cancel_child_failed` posture -> `src/server/handlers/plan-runs.ts` (drop the `:1453` line number); the `defaultSpawn` duplicate -> `src/server/main/utils.ts`; generic 'JSON shaping / payload shapes' references -> the `src/server/handlers/` directory.

name: Fix stale src/server/handlers.ts doc references after handler split

sections.context: Nightwatch patrol 2026-06-04 over a clean tree (bun run typecheck + biome check both green, 627 files). The codebase is mature and recently patrolled, so findings are narrow: two pockets of documentation drift left behind by the src/server/handlers.ts -> src/server/handlers/ directory split and by the WarrenTriggerKind enum work. (1) The completed plan pl-d6cd (warren-47e0) swept stale `handlers.ts` doc references but its acceptance grep deliberately excludes test files (`grep -v '.test.'`), so two test-comment references still point at the now-nonexistent monolith: `src/runs/spawn/seed-extensions.test.ts:127` cites `handlers.ts:354` and `src/preview/proxy/route-match.test.ts:117` says "adding a new API surface to handlers.ts". (2) The doc comment in `src/seeds-cli/warren-extensions.ts:22-25` misattributes the legacy `"manual-trigger"` writer: it claims `src/server/handlers/runs/index.ts` (Run Now) writes it, but Run Now (`createRunHandler` in `src/server/handlers/runs/dispatch.ts`) passes no `trigger` and falls through to the `"manual"` default; the only writer of `"manual-trigger"` is the cron-trigger manual-run handler at `src/server/handlers/projects.ts:188` (POST /projects/:id/triggers/:triggerId/run). The sibling comment in `src/runs/spawn/seed-extensions.ts:37` already attributes it correctly, confirming warren-extensions.ts is the drifted copy. All fixes are comment-only; no behavior, signatures, deps, or formatting of linter-clean code change.
sections.behavior_invariant: Runtime behavior, exported symbols, and public API signatures stay byte-for-byte identical — these are comment-only edits to test descriptions and a doc block. `bun test`, `bun run lint`, `bun run typecheck`, and `bun run check:all` must all stay green exactly as they are today. No executable `.ts`/`.tsx` line changes, no imports touched, no enum or schema changes. The `"manual-trigger"` string value emitted at projects.ts:188 and asserted by projects.settings.test.ts:453 is NOT changed (that would be a behavior change filed separately if ever pursued); only the prose that describes who writes it is corrected.
sections.approach: Two independent, comment-only PRs grouped by concern. Step 1 repoints the two stale `handlers.ts` test-comment references at their verified current homes (seed-extensions.test.ts -> the `"manual-trigger"` writer `src/server/handlers/projects.ts`; route-match.test.ts -> `src/server/handlers/index.ts`, the module its own code already imports). Step 2 corrects the misattribution in `src/seeds-cli/warren-extensions.ts` so it names `src/server/handlers/projects.ts` (the cron-trigger manual-run handler) as the `"manual-trigger"` writer and clarifies that Run Now (POST /runs) defaults to `"manual"`. Order is irrelevant; both gate the release step.

Step 2: `src/seeds-cli/warren-extensions.ts` lines 22-25 attribute the legacy `"manual-trigger"` string to `src/server/handlers/projects.ts` (cron-trigger manual-run handler) and state that Run Now (POST /runs) defaults to `"manual"`; the comment no longer claims `src/server/handlers/runs/index.ts` writes `"manual-trigger"`.
